title: Review: wolftpm pass 3
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — All P0/P1 issues from prior passes are closed. Remaining risks are minor inconsistencies (cast style) and a doc example that will bitrot without a CI check.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — One missed public type is missing Debug (EccKey), TpmRc not re-exported at crate root, and Error::check visibility is inconsistent. No unsafe soundness issues. No gratuitous clones, no unwraps in non-test production code, no Box<dyn Error> in API. The non_exhaustive, structured error enums, and SAFETY comments are all correct.
Confidence: 10% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: FMT1 stripping correctness (tested only implicitly by hardware test). Uncertainty driven by: cannot run hardware TPM tests in this environment; shim.c behavior depends on wolfTPM internals that can't be verified statically. Would raise confidence if: there were unit tests for FMT1 stripping with known rc values, and CI ran against a swtpm instance.
